mission.title: keda: Rabbitmq scaler: Allow providing separate paremeters for user and password
mission.description: Rabbitmq scaler: Allow providing separate paremeters for user and password . This issue affects 13+ users.

steps.title: Identify keda feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your keda deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=keda
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Rabbitmq scaler: Allow providing separate paremeters for user and password

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keda resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n <namespace>
```
### Proposal

I would like to be able to provide username/password as separate parameters for the RabbitMQ scaler, in order to be able to get host from Environment variable and username/password from

steps.title: Apply the fix for Rabbitmq scaler: Allow providing separate paremeters for use
steps.description:
Add the possibility to use username/password as separate parameters for the RabbitMQ scaler, as well as domain

### Checklist

Fixes #

Relates to #2513
```yaml
envFrom:
  - prefix: RABBITMQ_
    secretRef:
      name: example-deployment-default-user
env:
  - name: RABBITMQ_ConnectionString
    value: amqp://$(RABBITMQ_username):$(RABBITMQ_password)@$(RABBITMQ_host):$(RABBITMQ_port)
```

steps.title: Confirm Rabbitmq scaler: Allow providing separate paremete is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Add the possibility to use username/password as separate parameters for the RabbitMQ scaler, as well as domain

### Checklist

Fixes #

Relates to #2513. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

env:

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
